title: ServiceNow Inc. (NOW) Intrinsic Value — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of ServiceNow Inc. (NOW).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: ServiceNow Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 20% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.5% WACC balances ServiceNow Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.5% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisParagraphs: ServiceNow Inc. (NOW) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 20% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 10.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is ServiceNow Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 20% FCF growth and 10.5% WACC, Opus calculates NOW's intrinsic value at approximately $1090.41 per share, suggesting the stock is fairly valued at $1050.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive NOW's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (20%) and discount rate (10.5%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.
